title: Revealed: KTM's secretly derestricted and highly polluting motorcycles
subhead: The Austrian manufacturer KTM is facilitating the systematic removal of speed and power restrictions from some of its motorcycles in France and across Europe, making them illegal and highly polluting, according to the investigation 'Unrestricted' by a media consortium including Le Monde.

title: From a kidnapping in France to crypto wallet in Venezuela: Inside a ransom laundering scheme
Israeli strikes kill at least 31 in south Lebanon as Israel expands ground operations
'It was 30°C in the classrooms this afternoon': France experiences its hottest day in May on record
subhead: Temperatures soared on Tuesday, reaching between 33 and 36°C across much of France, especially in the West, which was 10 to 15°C above seasonal norms. These are midsummer temperatures, even though summer has not yet begun.

title: French presidential election: Is it that bad that the bench is so crowded?
subhead: While Gabriel Attal, Edouard Philippe and Bruno Retailleau are the three candidates in the running in the so-called 'central bloc,' many other hopefuls are waiting to seize an opportunity, notes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: Documentary follows Greta Thunberg and Swedish climate activists' hope and despair
subhead: For seven years, filmmaker Helena Molin followed a dozen teenagers who helped found the Fridays for Future movement in 2018. The resulting documentary has been met with critical acclaim.

title: Economists Zucman and Saez, the two 'Frenchies' campaigning to tax California's billionaires
subhead: California's 250 billionaires have amassed staggering wealth while paying very little in taxes, French researchers Gabriel Zucman and Emmanuel Saez denounced in an op-ed for the New York Times. They support a proposal for a one-time 5% wealth tax, which will be put to a referendum on November 3.

The network of US pilots helping women seek abortion care
subhead: Responding to increasingly strict anti-abortion laws in Texas, a non-profit organization offers free flights to neighboring states. The group also assists individuals seeking gender-affirming care.

title: The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
subhead: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.

title: How China uses influencers to polish its image
subhead: Chongqing, a futuristic Chinese city, has caught the eye of influencers and bloggers. Beijing, meanwhile, welcomes new visitors, often unaware of the geopolitical and ideological currents running beneath their social media posts.
